heading: Retirement Savings Lost and Found
label: 1153 Retirement Savings Lost and Found
notes: Change of NameCommittee on Education and Labor of House of Representatives changed to Committee on Education and the Workforce of House of Representatives by House Resolution No. 5, One Hundred Eighteenth Congress, .Jan. 9, 2023

children.label: A
children.text: allow an individual to search for information that enables the individual to locate the administrator of any plan described in paragraph (2) with respect to which the individual is or was a participant or beneficiary, and provide contact information for the administrator of any such plan;

children.label: B
children.text: allow the Secretary to assist such an individual in locating any such plan of the individual; and

children.label: C
children.text: allow the Secretary to make any necessary changes to contact information on record for the administrator based on any changes to the plan due to merger or consolidation of the plan with any other plan, division of the plan into two or more plans, bankruptcy, termination, change in name of the plan, change in name or address of the administrator, or other causes.

children.heading: Plans described
children.label: 2 Plans described
children.text: A plan described in this paragraph is a plan to which the vesting standards of apply.section 1053 of this title

children.heading: Administration
children.label: b Administration
children.text: The Retirement Savings Lost and Found established under subsection (a) shall provide individuals described in subsection (a)(1) only with the ability to search for information that enables the individual to locate the administrator and contact information for the administrator of any plan with respect to which the individual is or was a participant or beneficiary, sufficient to allow the individual to locate the individual’s plan in order to make a claim for benefits owing to the individual under the plan.

children.heading: Safeguarding participant privacy and security
children.label: c Safeguarding participant privacy and security

children.text: ensure that individuals’ plan and personal information maintained by the Retirement Savings Lost and Found is protected; and
allow any individual to contact the Secretary to opt out of inclusion in the Retirement Savings Lost and Found.

children.heading: Definition of administrator
children.label: d Definition of administrator
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “administrator” has the meaning given such term in .section 1002(16)(A) of this title
the information described in paragraphs (1) through (4) of ;section 6057(b) of title 26
the information described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of ;section 6057(a)(2) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: who, during the current plan year or any previous plan year, was reported under , and with respect to whom the benefits described in clause (ii) thereof were fully paid during the plan year;section 6057(a)(2)(C) of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to whom any amount was distributed under during the plan year; orsection 401(a)(31)(B) of title 26

children.label: C
children.text: with respect to whom a deferred annuity contract was distributed during the plan year; and

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a participant described in subparagraph (B) thereof, the name and address of the designated trustee or issuer described in and the account number of the individual retirement plan to which the amount was distributed; andsection 401(a)(31)(B)(i) of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a participant described in subparagraph (C) thereof, the name and address of the issuer of such annuity contract and the contract or certificate number.
may use or disclose information collected under this section only for the purpose described in subsection (a)(1)(B), and
may disclose such information only to such employees of the Department of Labor whose official duties relate to the purpose described in such subsection.
conduct an audit of the administration of the Retirement Savings Lost and Found; and

submit a report on such audit to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions and the Committee on Finance of the Senate and the Committee on Ways and Means and the Committee on Education and Labor of the House of Representatives.
